elements.title: FRA DOTX 219
elements.contents: ... that data collected by FRA DOTX 219 (pictured) have been used in investigations of train derailments?

elements.title: an eight-track EP lasting less than nine minutes
elements.contents: ... that an eight-track EP lasting less than nine minutes was re-released ten years after the death of its performer in a fire?

elements.title: Women Without Men
elements.contents: ... that shortly after the publication of Women Without Men in Iran, it was banned and its author imprisoned?

elements.title: Star Channel
elements.contents: ... that when Canada's Star Channel closed with three hours' warning, some cable companies resorted to pirating a competitor's signal?

elements.title: her murder in 2015
elements.contents: ... that Stephanie Scott's favourite colour became a symbol of national remembrance across Australia following her murder in 2015?

elements.title: Benjamin Hammar House
elements.contents: ... that the families of the neighboring Benjamin Hammar House and Samuel Dyer House shared both meals and tools while they restored their historic homes?

elements.title: a 1647 evening song
elements.contents: ... that "Now all the woods are sleeping" is an 1865 Catherine Winkworth translation of a 1647 evening song once mocked as foolish by Frederick the Great?

elements.title: Fuente de Petróleos
elements.contents: ... that the Fuente de Petróleos includes self-portraits of its architect and sculptor as oil workers?

elements.title: Bum
elements.contents: ... that Bum was known to club other dogs with his leg stump?
